approach: Codec-only fix: expand X..Y ranges eagerly into {kind:char} store items in parseStoreItems (range check before the SMP early-bail so astral ranges expand too); emit re-collapses ascending runs (len>=3) back to X..Y. No @keyboard-studio/contracts change (IR option A). Descending/malformed ranges preserve-opaque with new descending-range/malformed-range reasons, mirroring kmcmplib; equal-endpoint stays lenient (one item) per user, oracle backstops. buildProducedSet and all consumers inherit the fix unchanged.

verified.what: engine codec suite (parse/emit/roundtrip + 20 new range tests)

verified.result: 1738 passed, 1 pre-existing recognizer/integration fail (NCAPS modifier, corpus-version, unrelated to ranges - confirmed failing at baseline via stash)
0 semantic mismatches; 0 codepoint-range raw dot-dot items (108 remaining raw are all vkey ranges, out of scope)

casing undetermined 15->8 (7 historic-script range keyboards incl takri_inscript now content-derived); encoding 46 unchanged (non-range adiga_danef case, tracked separately); byte-identical rebuilds; lint GREEN
km-verification PASS; km-synthesis FITS (no blockers, no contracts change); km-qc PASS 95/100 no P0. P1 doc-drift + P2 mixed-endpoint FIXED; residual P2 (4 near-identical decode blocks) left as-is

decisions.decision: {"what":"emit re-collapse skips all-printable-ASCII ascending runs","why":"the raw >=3 ascending+1 rule would mangle dictionary/&word stores (existing store(word) abc test) and ASCII alphabet stores into codepoint ranges, contradicting FR-008 human-legible goal; all concrete contract examples (Devanagari/SMP/Hebrew) are non-ASCII so still collapse. Round-trip stays semantic-stable."}

{"what":"vkey-bracket range [K_A]..[K_Z] is NOT treated as a codepoint range","why":"neither endpoint decodes as a codepoint; classifyRange returns null so &CasedKeys vkey ranges keep legacy vkey/raw/vkey items instead of opaquing the store. A codepoint range needs at least one decodable endpoint; the C9 malformed cases all have one."}

concerns.note: {"what":"detectRangeAt has 4 near-identical decode+consumed blocks (parse.ts)","severity":"P2","disposition":"left as-is: each path is well-commented and independently tested (C1-C9); refactor risk outweighs benefit late in the cycle"}
